mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer income, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in >65% of observations during expansion periods, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 2 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher real discount rates, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of months following inflation spikes above the threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction in a logistic regression with p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit expansion, which immediately lifts equity risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters following positive GDP revisions, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and falling corporate output, which reduces earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of months following the production contraction threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.
